accepted.reason: Fractal escape-time loops are classic numerical iteration kernels suitable for optimization tasks.
Radial histogram accumulation is a reusable aggregation kernel with nested loop structure.
Decision stump threshold scoring is a practical ML optimization primitive.
Power-iteration spectral norm estimation is a high-value linear algebra kernel.
2D convolution is a canonical dense compute kernel for Cython speedup tasks.
Stream compaction scan/select is a fundamental data-parallel primitive.

skipped.reason: Cython add-loop test case is compiler behavior coverage, not a standalone optimization workload.
Unicode function behavior tests are runtime checks with low transfer for optimization training.
Context-manager inlining tests are not algorithmic optimization exemplars.
Specialized utility conversion code appears domain-specific and less reusable for GRPO training.
